methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Mind Robotics, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by RJ Scaringe with $1.015B raised to date, is valued against a comparables universe of 9 peers: 1 public company (ticker 277810.KQ) and 8 private robotics firms including 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and Fairmat.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $1.0B total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B to next-stage advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $1.9B. The peer-median method examines 6 same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $1.1B, though the peer set exhibits wide dispersion (range: $300M–$10.0B), with 1X Technologies as a notable outlier at $10.0B.

These methods converge on a triangulated valuation range of $914M (low) to $2.2B (high), with a median of $1.5B. The $914M floor reflects a discount to raised capital; the $2.2B ceiling reflects the stage-progression uplift. Given the 33x spread in peer valuations and the absence of meaningful revenue or profitability data in the provided inputs, valuation precision is limited. Key uncertainties center on Mind Robotics' relative technology maturity, go-to-market traction, and capital efficiency versus the peer cohort.
